# Nuclideon udSDK Development Agreement

> [!IMPORTANT]
> This Agreement is a legal agreement between you and Nuclideon Pty Ltd (ACN 681 220 548) (**Nuclideon**, **us** or **we**) and governs your use of the Nuclideon udSDK.
>
> BY INDICATING YOUR ACCEPTANCE, DOWNLOADING, INSTALLING OR USING THE SDK, YOU ACCEPT ALL THE TERMS AND CONDITIONS OF THIS AGREEMENT ON BEHALF OF YOURSELF AND, WHERE APPLICABLE, YOUR EMPLOYER OR THE ENTITY YOU REPRESENT. If you do not agree to these terms, do not download, install or use the SDK.

---

## 1. Definitions

In this Agreement:

- **Agreement** means this Nuclideon udSDK Development Agreement, as amended from time to time in accordance with clause 15.
- **Confidential Information** means the SDK and any non-public technical information, data formats, protocols, or documentation relating to the SDK disclosed by Nuclideon to Developer.
- **Derivative Product** means a software application or service created by Developer that incorporates the SDK in compiled or linked form, such that the SDK itself is not separately extractable or usable by an end user of that application or service.
- **Developer** means the individual or legal entity accepting this Agreement. Where acceptance is by an individual on behalf of an employer or other entity, Developer means that employer or entity.
- **Documentation** means the technical documentation, API reference, and usage guides provided by Nuclideon with or for the SDK.
- **Sample Code** means any example source code files provided by Nuclideon as part of the SDK package, identified as sample or example code.
- **SDK** means the Nuclideon udSDK software, including compiled libraries, header files, Tools, API Information, Documentation, and related materials, but excluding Sample Code unless expressly stated.

---

## 2. Grant of Licence

a) Subject to the terms of this Agreement, Nuclideon grants Developer a limited, non-exclusive, non-transferable, non-sublicensable licence to:

   - install and use the SDK solely to develop and test Derivative Products; and
   - incorporate the SDK in compiled or linked form into Derivative Products for distribution to end users.

b) **Sample Code**: Nuclideon grants Developer a separate, non-exclusive, perpetual licence to copy, modify, and incorporate Sample Code into Developer's own products and projects without restriction, provided that any such use does not constitute redistribution of the SDK itself.

c) **No Other Rights**: Except as expressly stated in this clause 2, no other rights are granted. All rights not expressly granted are reserved by Nuclideon.

---

## 3. Restrictions

Developer must not:

a) distribute, sell, sublicense, rent, lease, lend, or otherwise transfer the SDK (or any portion of it) as a standalone product or component separately from a Derivative Product;

b) make the SDK available to any third party except as incorporated into a Derivative Product in accordance with clause 2;

c) except to the extent expressly permitted by applicable law and only for interoperability purposes: decompile, reverse engineer, disassemble, decrypt, or attempt to derive the source code of any compiled component of the SDK;

d) circumvent or attempt to circumvent any technological protection measures used by Nuclideon in respect of the SDK;

e) modify, translate, or create derivative works of the SDK itself (as distinct from Derivative Products);

f) remove, alter, or obscure any proprietary notices, labels, or marks on the SDK;

g) use the SDK to develop a product that competes directly with Nuclideon's core SDK or point cloud platform offerings;

h) use the SDK in connection with any application intended to enable unlawful surveillance, weapons targeting, or other uses prohibited by applicable law.

---

## 4. Attribution

All Derivative Products must include the following attribution in their documentation, about screen, or credits (as applicable):

> Powered by Nuclideon (nuclideon.com)

---

## 5. Intellectual Property Rights

a) Nuclideon is and remains at all times the sole owner of the SDK and all intellectual property rights in it. Nothing in this Agreement grants or assigns to Developer any ownership interest or intellectual property rights in the SDK other than the licence rights set out in clause 2.

b) Developer acknowledges that it has no right, title, or interest in the intellectual property rights in the SDK.

c) **Patent licence**: Subject to the terms of this Agreement, Nuclideon grants Developer a non-exclusive, non-transferable, non-sublicensable licence under any patents owned or controlled by Nuclideon that are necessarily infringed by Developer's use of the SDK in accordance with this Agreement. This patent licence does not extend to any use of the SDK beyond the scope of clause 2, and does not include rights to any patents covering Developer's own Derivative Products.

d) **Feedback**: If Developer provides Nuclideon with any feedback, suggestions, or bug reports regarding the SDK (**Feedback**), Developer grants Nuclideon a perpetual, irrevocable, royalty-free, worldwide licence to use and incorporate that Feedback into Nuclideon's products and services without obligation to Developer.

---

## 6. Confidentiality

a) Developer must keep Confidential Information confidential and must not disclose it to any third party without Nuclideon's prior written consent.

b) Developer may disclose Confidential Information only to its employees and contractors who have a need to know for the purposes of developing Derivative Products under this Agreement, and who are bound by confidentiality obligations no less protective than those in this clause.

c) Confidentiality obligations do not apply to information that: (i) is or becomes publicly available through no act or omission of Developer; (ii) was already known to Developer at the time of disclosure; or (iii) is required to be disclosed by law or court order, provided Developer gives Nuclideon reasonable prior notice.

---

## 7. Security and Risk

Developer is responsible for protecting the SDK and any related materials from unauthorised access, use, or damage, and must implement reasonable technical and organisational measures to prevent unauthorised access to the SDK.

---

## 8. Export Controls

Developer must comply with all applicable export control and sanctions laws and regulations, including those of Australia, the United States, and any other relevant jurisdiction, in connection with its use and distribution of the SDK and any Derivative Products. Developer must not export, re-export, or transfer the SDK to any destination, end user, or end use prohibited under applicable export control laws without obtaining all required government authorisations.

---

## 9. Warranty Disclaimer

a) THE SDK IS PROVIDED "AS IS." TO THE MAXIMUM EXTENT PERMITTED BY LAW, NUCLIDEON DISCLAIMS ALL WARRANTIES, EXPRESS OR IMPLIED, INCLUDING ALL IMPLIED WARRANTIES OF MERCHANTABILITY, FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE, AND NON-INFRINGEMENT.

b) Where applicable law (including the Australian Consumer Law) implies guarantees that cannot be excluded, Nuclideon's liability for breach of such a guarantee is limited, to the extent permitted by law, to: (i) in the case of goods, repair, replacement, or refund of the cost of the goods; and (ii) in the case of services, re-supply of the services or payment of the cost of having the services re-supplied.

c) Nuclideon is under no obligation to provide support, maintenance, upgrades, or future versions of the SDK under this Agreement.

---

## 10. Limitation of Liability

a) **Exclusion of consequential loss**: To the maximum extent permitted by law, in no event will Nuclideon or its officers, employees, contractors, or subcontractors be liable for any indirect, incidental, special, consequential, or punitive damages, including loss of profits, loss of data, business interruption, or loss of goodwill, arising out of or in connection with this Agreement or the use of the SDK, even if advised of the possibility of such damages.

b) **Cap on direct damages**: Nuclideon's total aggregate liability to Developer for all direct damages arising under or in connection with this Agreement will not exceed the greater of: (i) the total fees paid by Developer to Nuclideon under this Agreement in the twelve (12) months preceding the event giving rise to the claim; or (ii) AUD $500.

c) **Basis of bargain**: Developer acknowledges that Nuclideon has set its fees and entered into this Agreement in reliance on the limitations and exclusions of liability set out in this clause, and that those limitations and exclusions reflect a reasonable allocation of risk between the parties.

d) **Indemnification**: Developer will indemnify, defend, and hold harmless Nuclideon and its officers, employees, and contractors from and against any claims, damages, losses, and expenses (including reasonable legal fees) arising out of or relating to: (i) Developer's breach of this Agreement; (ii) Developer's Derivative Products; or (iii) Developer's violation of any applicable law.

---

## 11. Termination

a) **Termination for breach**: Nuclideon may terminate this Agreement immediately by written notice if Developer materially breaches this Agreement and, where the breach is capable of remedy, fails to remedy it within thirty (30) days of written notice from Nuclideon specifying the breach.

b) **Termination for insolvency**: Nuclideon may terminate this Agreement immediately by written notice if Developer becomes insolvent, makes an assignment for the benefit of creditors, or has a receiver, administrator, or liquidator appointed.

c) **Termination by Developer**: Developer may terminate this Agreement at any time by ceasing all use of the SDK and destroying all copies in its possession or control.

d) **Effect of termination**: Upon termination of this Agreement:

   - all licences granted under clause 2 will immediately cease;
   - Developer must immediately cease all use of the SDK and destroy or return all copies of the SDK in its possession or control;
   - Developer may continue to distribute Derivative Products that were completed and commercially released prior to the termination date for a period not exceeding ninety (90) days, after which all distribution must cease.

e) **Survival**: Clauses 1, 3, 5, 6, 9, 10, 11(d), 11(e), 12, 13, and 14 survive termination of this Agreement.

---

## 12. Assignment

Developer may not assign, transfer, or delegate any rights or obligations under this Agreement without Nuclideon's prior written consent, except that Developer may assign this Agreement in its entirety to a successor entity in connection with a merger, acquisition, or sale of substantially all of Developer's assets, provided the successor entity agrees in writing to be bound by all terms of this Agreement. Any purported assignment in violation of this clause is void.

---

## 13. Governing Law and Jurisdiction

This Agreement is governed by the laws of Queensland, Australia, without regard to its conflict of law principles. Each party irrevocably submits to the non-exclusive jurisdiction of the courts of Queensland, Australia. The United Nations Convention on Contracts for the International Sale of Goods does not apply to this Agreement.

---

## 14. General

a) **Entire agreement**: This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the parties with respect to the SDK and supersedes all prior agreements, representations, and understandings.

b) **Severability**: If any provision of this Agreement is held unenforceable, that provision will be modified to the minimum extent necessary to make it enforceable, and the remaining provisions will continue in full force and effect.

c) **Waiver**: Failure by either party to enforce any provision of this Agreement will not constitute a waiver of future enforcement.

d) **Relationship**: The parties are independent contractors. Nothing in this Agreement creates any employment, partnership, agency, or joint venture relationship.

---

## 15. Updates to This Agreement

Nuclideon may update this Agreement by publishing a new version at nuclideon.com/sdk-license. Continued use of the SDK after the effective date of an updated version constitutes acceptance of the updated terms. Nuclideon will provide at least thirty (30) days' notice of material changes by email or through the Nuclideon developer portal where practicable.

---
